% Remote107 figure snippets — 2026-05-13
% Generated by Claude. Place in paper2 manuscript where appropriate.

% Figure 1: 410M Selective KV PPL comparison
\begin{figure}[htbp]
\centering
\includegraphics[width=0.7\columnwidth]{figures/fig_remote107_selective_kv_ppl_20260513.pdf}
\caption{Pythia-410M selective analog KV-cache PPL comparison (claim-lock protocol). Digital baseline PPL=23.30; last1 (layer~23) reaches 18.81; last2 (layers~22--23) reaches 18.67; all24 degrades to 25.98.}
\label{fig:remote107_selective_kv}
\end{figure}

% Figure 2: p28b/p69b downstream task comparison
\begin{figure}[htbp]
\centering
\includegraphics[width=0.9\columnwidth]{figures/fig_remote107_downstream_comparison_20260513.pdf}
\caption{Downstream task accuracy (LAMBADA, HellaSwag, ARC-Easy) for Pythia-2.8B and Pythia-6.9B: clean vs analog KV. Degradation is within $\pm0.5$ pp for all tasks.}
\label{fig:remote107_downstream}
\end{figure}

% Figure 3: D2D/C2C robustness sweep
\begin{figure}[htbp]
\centering
\includegraphics[width=0.95\columnwidth]{figures/fig_remote107_robustness_20260513.pdf}
\caption{D2D and C2C robustness sweeps for Pythia-2.8B and Pythia-6.9B. (a)~Fixed $\sigma_{\mathrm{C2C}}=0.01$, sweep $\sigma_{\mathrm{D2D}}$. (b)~Fixed $\sigma_{\mathrm{D2D}}=0.02$, sweep $\sigma_{\mathrm{C2C}}$.}
\label{fig:remote107_robustness}
\end{figure}

% Figure 4: R3 adaptive schedule comparison
\begin{figure}[htbp]
\centering
\includegraphics[width=0.88\columnwidth]{figures/fig_remote107_r3_adaptive_ppl_20260513.pdf}
\caption{Adaptive noise schedule comparison across model scales. For the frozen-target 410M runs, cosine decay gives the lowest post-HAT PPL (18.31) versus fixed (18.75), layer-wise (18.36), and reverse layer-wise (21.30). At 2.8B and 6.9B, fixed, cosine, and layer-wise remain within a narrow band (12.68--12.70 and 11.38--11.43), indicating that schedule sensitivity weakens as the model scale increases.}
\label{fig:remote107_r3_adaptive}
\end{figure}

% Figure 5: p28b layer/schedule ablation
\begin{figure}[htbp]
\centering
\includegraphics[width=0.82\columnwidth]{figures/fig_remote107_r3_p28b_ablation_20260513.pdf}
\caption{Pythia-2.8B layer-scope and schedule ablation. The last2+cosine configuration reaches the best PPL (12.56), improving over last2 fixed (13.78) and slightly outperforming last1 fixed (12.68). This indicates that adaptive schedules matter more when the analog scope expands beyond the single terminal layer.}
\label{fig:remote107_r3_p28b_ablation}
\end{figure}
